source.definition: A domesticated mammal, usually with four legs, commonly kept as a pet and often serving as a companion to humans.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "dog" has an uncertain origin but has been used since Middle English to refer to a domesticated canine. Its exact etymology is unclear, with some theories suggesting it may derive from a now-lost Old English word.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „dog” are o origine incertă, fiind folosit încă din engleza mijlocie pentru a desemna un câine domestic. Etimologia sa exactă nu este clară, unele teorii sugerând că ar putea deriva dintr-un cuvânt vechi englez pierdut.

target.definition: Un mamifer domestic, de obicei patruped, ținut ca animal de companie și adesea folosit ca însoțitor al omului.

explanation.sourceText: The origin of the Romanian word "câine" is unclear; it is often considered a word of unknown or possibly Dacian origin, as it does not derive directly from the Latin "canis".
explanation.targetText: Originea cuvântului românesc „câine” este incertă; acesta nu derivă direct din latinescul „canis”, fiind considerat de mulți lingviști ca având origine necunoscută sau posibil dacică.

examples.sourceText: The dog runs in the garden.

learningTips.text: The Romanian word "câine" sounds a bit like "kine" – imagine a dog in a small cage ('cage') to help you remember.
Remember the Latin root 'canis' which appears in many Romance languages like Italian 'cane' or French 'chien' to connect with the Romanian "câine".
Think of the English word 'canine' which relates to dogs and sounds similar to the Romanian "câine".
Imagine a dog calling you 'Kine!' as if it's saying its own name to help memorize the word.
